name: healthcare_water_allocation_model_kestrel_auto_ben
premise: A Healthcare clinical command center is in a fast-moving incident involving healthcare water allocation model kestrel auto ben. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Every delay increases patient safety, triage integrity, and privacy. Stakeholders are already framing this as autonomy vs beneficence under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Zara Petrov
agents.identity: I am Zara Petrov, accountable for a defensible call in Healthcare when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays beneficence for autonomy.

agents.name: Leila Patel
agents.identity: I am Leila Patel, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Healthcare.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so beneficence isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of autonomy.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect beneficence.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Evidence is partial. Zara Petrov must decide under autonomy vs beneficence. What does Zara Petrov do?
